title: HTTP Services, Clients, Boundaries, Security, and Graceful Shutdown · Runtime Contract: Service Errors Observability
summary: A small service accepts hostile input, calls a slow dependency, serves concurrent requests, and must shut down without losing work. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go service errors observability increment in a resilient secure HTTP service design. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map internal failures to stable external responses while retaining structured correlation, cause, latency, and privacy-safe logs.
Validate untrusted input, escape output for its sink, minimize authority and data, protect secrets, and set explicit server resource limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Service Errors Observability · Theory Service Errors Observability
steps.instruction:
theory case — Service request 2 has a hostile label, oversized body, slow dependency, duplicate header, and client cancellation during shutdown. Bound resources, validate and encode by sink, preserve stable responses, and drain owned work without leaking internals.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Service Errors Observability contract for the supplied data.
